problem:
Bug: SSRF bypass in validate_safe_url when LANGCHAIN_ENV=local_test. I'm auditing the SSRF protection layer in langchain-core.

I expect validate_safe_url() to always block untrusted hostnames.

Instead, when LANGCHAIN_ENV=local_test, any hostname matching
startswith("test") and "server" in hostname bypasses all validation
and is returned unchecked — including attacker-controlled hostnames
like test.attacker.server.com.

A second issue: _effective_allowed_hosts() uses startswith("local")
which e (npm config set registry is not working — it seems correct per old docs)
memory_hint: Use npmrc for registry config. The old syntax was removed.
failure_without_memory: Keeps using npm config set registry which is deprecated. Endless debugging.
